Class TermFactory

java.lang.Object
de.uka.ilkd.key.logic.TermFactory
public final class TermFactory extends Object
The TermFactory is the only way to create terms using constructors of class Term or any of its subclasses. It is the only class that implements and may exploit knowledge about sub classes of JTerm. All other classes of the system only know about terms what the JTerm class offers them. This class is used to encapsulate knowledge about the internal term structures. See TermBuilder for more convenient methods to create terms.

    • createTerm

      public JTerm createTerm(@NonNull Operator op, ImmutableArray<JTerm> subs, ImmutableArray<QuantifiableVariable> boundVars, ImmutableArray<TermLabel> labels)
      Master method for term creation. Should be the only place where terms are created in the entire system.

    • createTerm

      public @NonNull JTerm createTerm(@NonNull Operator junctor, @NonNull List<JTerm> terms)
      Reduce the given list of terms into a one term by using the operator. The reduction is left-associative. e.g., the result is ((a op b) op c) op d .
      Parameters:
      junctor - the left-associative operator to combine the terms together
      terms - a list of non-null temrs

    • createTermWithOrigin

      public JTerm createTermWithOrigin(JTerm t, String origin)
